title: What if Eva was a generic action robot show
display_title: What if Eva was a generic action robot show
wikitext:
[]: What if Eva was a generic action robot show?             Quote Post by GAP » Tue Dec 30, 2008 4:11 am I am not sure if there any thread like this already but I was wondering what would happen if Eva was just another generic action show with cardboard cutout personalities? In what ways would it be the same? In what ways it could be different?

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Tue Dec 30, 2008 4:14 am Well, for one, this forum would've been undoubtedly devoted to something else. Second, it wouldn't be so popular OR revolutionary.

[]:             Quote Post by Nasmoe » Tue Dec 30, 2008 4:49 am I wouldn't still be fixated on it

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Tue Dec 30, 2008 8:23 am I never would have watched it in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 30, 2008 8:53 am Well, for one, this forum would've been undoubtedly devoted to something else.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Dec 30, 2008 9:05 am EDIT: Blank post.

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Tue Dec 30, 2008 9:20 am Shinji would have to yell into a watch to summon his unit.

[]: Re: What if Eva was a generic action robot show?             Quote Post by pat457 » Tue Dec 30, 2008 11:05 am GAP wrote: I am not sure if there any thread like this already but I was wondering what would happen if Eva was just another generic action show with cardboard cutout personalities? In what ways would it be the same? In what ways it could be different?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Dec 30, 2008 11:15 am It would probably have been called Gurren Lagann

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Dec 30, 2008 12:02 pm Mr. Tines wrote: It would probably have been called Gurren Lagann

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Dec 30, 2008 12:10 pm Mr. Tines wrote: It would probably have been called Gurren Lagann

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Dec 30, 2008 12:24 pm BrikHaus wrote: Mr. Tines wrote: It would probably have been called Gurren Lagann

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Dec 30, 2008 1:55 pm If it was a generic mecha show, I wouldn't be the obsessed fangirl I am today. (I probably wouldn't have given the show the time of day.) Besides hearing that the show was psychological, the other reason I got into the show was because I also heard it was different from the norm.

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Tue Dec 30, 2008 3:02 pm Mr. Tines wrote: It would probably have been called Gurren Lagann

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Wed Dec 31, 2008 2:52 am That may work!

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Dec 31, 2008 10:20 am Evangelion was the first mecha series I ever watched, so I suppose even if it was generic, I still would have watched it.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Dec 31, 2008 10:22 am Mr. Tines wrote: It would probably have been called Gurren Lagann

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Dec 31, 2008 11:38 am We wouldn't be here.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Thu Jan 01, 2009 2:17 pm It is good thing that it isn't a generic robot action show otherwise we wouldn't have all these great discussions about the characters psyche, metaphysics and all this other stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Thu Jan 01, 2009 2:20 pm Kaworu would be male, and people like us would roam across the barren lands for something deeper than Generic Eva.
summary:
[]: What if Eva was a generic action robot show?             Quote Post by GAP » Tue Dec 30, 2008 4:11 am I am not sure if there any thread like this already but I was wondering what would happen if Eva was just another generic action show with cardboard cutout personalities? In what ways would it be the same? In what ways it could be different?

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX &ra
